forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,49 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8880'
+title: Cache embedded builtin libraries (prompts/skills/agents) once per process to fix slow MCP server startup
+---
+## What
+
+**Root cause** (investigated 2026-05-21) of the cluster of `#test-failure` in-process-MCP-server timeouts: every call to `start_mcp_server_with_options` (`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/unified_server.rs`) builds a fresh `McpServer` (`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs`) that re-parses the **embedded, identical builtin data** from scratch on every instance:
+
+- `McpServer::init_skill_library()` → `SkillLibrary::load_defaults()` → `SkillResolver::new().resolve_all()` (`crates/swissarmyhammer-skills/src/skill_library.rs:20`)
+- `McpServer::init_agent_library()` → `AgentLibrary::load_defaults()`
+- `McpServer::initialize()` → `PromptResolver::load_all_prompts()` → `load_builtin_prompts()` + per-file frontmatter/liquid parsing (`crates/swissarmyhammer-prompts/src/prompt_resolver.rs:45`)
+
+This is pure CPU work on static embedded constants, repeated per server instance and never cached. A single startup is tolerable (~seconds), but a full-workspace `cargo nextest run` (~13.7k tests) schedules a tail cluster of ~10 server-startup tests concurrently on a CPU-oversubscribed machine, so each instance's startup CPU is starved and exceeds the 300s nextest timeout. Confirmed: `test_client_list_tools` (in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/test_utils.rs`) times out under load **despite already passing a tempdir `working_dir`** — proving the residual cost is the per-instance builtin parse, not the monorepo walk that `working_dir` already eliminates.
+
+This affects production too: the kanban app starts a per-board in-process MCP server, and `sah serve` starts one per invocation — each pays the full re-parse.
+
+**Fix**: parse each embedded builtin library exactly once per process and reuse it cheaply across server instances.
+
+- Builtin prompts, skills, and agents are compiled-in static data (`include_dir!`/`include_str!`-style embedding) — identical for the life of the process. Memoize the parsed result behind a process-wide `OnceCell`/`once_cell::sync::Lazy` (or `std::sync::OnceLock`), and have each `McpServer` instance clone/share the cached parsed library (e.g. behind an `Arc`) instead of re-resolving.
+- Scope the cache to the **builtin/embedded portion only**. The filesystem layers (user `~/.swissarmyhammer`, local `./.swissarmyhammer`, VFS `load_all`) MUST still be loaded per-instance/per-working-dir, because those vary by HOME and cwd and tests rely on `IsolatedTestEnvironment` swapping HOME. Only the embedded-constant parse is safe to memoize.
+- Verify thread-safety: the cache is read concurrently by many server startups; the memoized value must be immutable (or `Arc`-shared read-only) after first init.
+
+### Files likely involved
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-prompts/src/prompt_resolver.rs` — cache `load_builtin_prompts()` output.
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-skills/src/skill_library.rs` — cache `SkillResolver::resolve_all()` (builtin portion).
+- The agent-library equivalent (find `AgentLibrary::load_defaults` / its resolver).
+- Possibly `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs` if the sharing seam belongs at `McpServer` construction.
+
+### Relationship to the open #test-failure tasks
+These symptom tasks are all the same root cause and should be re-verified (and likely closed) once this lands: `01KS3TQJ6KT96XH5TR5PY84GC8` (mcp_integration trio — already has the `working_dir` isolation fix applied, serial guards + nextest override removed), `01KS3TQCM3GG44GD4419D6CVXB` (done, mcp_integration basic), `01KS57J5F5YJ3QSB34Q139221R` (test_client_list_tools), `01KS57J8PPWDF20CQ9R3ES8XRP` (kanban-app open-board), and possibly `01KS3TQQTM404TGYFZ9E39EXP3` / `01KS5B9ZH604586D1AHC3NVPFJ` (file_watcher / entity watcher — confirm whether they share this cause or are notify-event-driven, which is different).
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] The embedded builtin prompt/skill/agent libraries are parsed at most once per process; constructing N `McpServer` instances does not re-parse the embedded data N times (assert via a counter/instrumentation test or a timing test).
+- [ ] Filesystem (user/local/VFS) prompt/skill/agent layers are still loaded per-instance and continue to respect `IsolatedTestEnvironment`'s HOME override — no cross-test bleed of user data.
+- [ ] A single in-process MCP server startup with an isolated `working_dir` measurably drops in CPU/wall-time vs. before (record before/after for one startup).
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run --workspace` completes with the in-process MCP-server-startup tests passing under load **without** per-test `serial_test` guards or the `mcp_server` `slow-timeout` override in `.config/nextest.toml` (i.e. the workarounds stay removed).
+- [ ] No behavior change to which prompts/skills/agents a server exposes (builtin + filesystem precedence preserved).
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Unit test in the cache's home crate proving the embedded library is parsed once: instrument the builtin-parse path with an `AtomicUsize` call counter (test-only) and assert it is 1 after constructing several libraries; OR a `OnceLock`-identity test asserting repeated calls return the same cached `Arc`.
+- [ ] Regression test that an `IsolatedTestEnvironment` with a custom builtin-overriding filesystem prompt still sees its filesystem prompt (cache does not shadow filesystem layers).
+- [ ] Re-run the previously-timing-out tests in isolation to confirm they still pass fast: `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-cli -E 'test(test_mcp_server_basic_functionality) | test(test_mcp_server_prompt_loading) | test(test_mcp_server_builtin_prompts)'` and `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-tools -E 'test(test_client_list_tools)'`.
+- [ ] Full-workspace verification: `cargo nextest run --workspace` completes with zero timeouts in the MCP-server-startup cluster, with the `.config/nextest.toml` `mcp_server` override absent and no `serial_test` guards on those tests. Record honest timings.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write the once-per-process parse-count test first, then introduce the cache, then confirm the symptom tests pass under load with the workarounds removed.
\ No newline at end of file

--- original
+++ modified
@@ -7,43 +7,39 @@

 ## What

-**Root cause** (investigated 2026-05-21) of the cluster of `#test-failure` in-process-MCP-server timeouts: every call to `start_mcp_server_with_options` (`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/unified_server.rs`) builds a fresh `McpServer` (`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs`) that re-parses the **embedded, identical builtin data** from scratch on every instance:
+**Root cause** (investigated 2026-05-21) of the cluster of `#test-failure` in-process-MCP-server timeouts: every call to `start_mcp_server_with_options` builds a fresh `McpServer` that re-parses the **embedded, identical builtin data** from scratch on every instance (skills/agents/prompts builtin parse). Under full-workspace nextest a tail cluster of concurrent server-startup tests starved CPU and timed out at 300s.

-- `McpServer::init_skill_library()` → `SkillLibrary::load_defaults()` → `SkillResolver::new().resolve_all()` (`crates/swissarmyhammer-skills/src/skill_library.rs:20`)
-- `McpServer::init_agent_library()` → `AgentLibrary::load_defaults()`
-- `McpServer::initialize()` → `PromptResolver::load_all_prompts()` → `load_builtin_prompts()` + per-file frontmatter/liquid parsing (`crates/swissarmyhammer-prompts/src/prompt_resolver.rs:45`)
+**Fix (implemented)**: parse each embedded builtin library exactly once per process behind a `once_cell::sync::Lazy`, and have each resolver clone/share the cached parse. Filesystem (user/local/VFS) layers still load per-instance so `IsolatedTestEnvironment` HOME isolation keeps working.

-This is pure CPU work on static embedded constants, repeated per server instance and never cached. A single startup is tolerable (~seconds), but a full-workspace `cargo nextest run` (~13.7k tests) schedules a tail cluster of ~10 server-startup tests concurrently on a CPU-oversubscribed machine, so each instance's startup CPU is starved and exceeds the 300s nextest timeout. Confirmed: `test_client_list_tools` (in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/test_utils.rs`) times out under load **despite already passing a tempdir `working_dir`** — proving the residual cost is the per-instance builtin parse, not the monorepo walk that `working_dir` already eliminates.
+## Implementation summary
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-skills/src/skill_resolver.rs`: added `static BUILTIN_SKILLS: Lazy<HashMap<String, Skill>>` parsed once via `parse_builtin_skills()`; `load_builtins()` now clones from the cache. Test-only `AtomicUsize` parse counter.
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-agents/src/agent_resolver.rs`: same pattern with `BUILTIN_AGENTS`.
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-prompts/src/prompt_resolver.rs`: added `static BUILTIN_PROMPTS: Lazy<Vec<ResolvedPrompt>>`; extracted `resolve_file_into_prompts()` shared by the cache builder and the filesystem path; `load_all_prompts()` applies cached builtins first (least precedence), then walks only the filesystem VFS and overrides by name. Builtins are no longer added to the per-instance VFS. Added `once_cell` dep.
+- No change to which prompts/skills/agents a server exposes; filesystem-overrides-builtin precedence preserved (verified by new + existing tests).

-This affects production too: the kanban app starts a per-board in-process MCP server, and `sah serve` starts one per invocation — each pays the full re-parse.
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [x] Embedded builtin prompt/skill/agent libraries parsed at most once per process (counter tests assert exactly 1 after constructing N resolvers).
+- [x] Filesystem layers still loaded per-instance and respect `IsolatedTestEnvironment` HOME override (new `test_filesystem_prompt_overrides_cached_builtin` proves a user prompt still overrides a cached builtin; existing user/local override tests still pass).
+- [x] Single startup embedded-parse cost measurably drops. Throwaway micro-bench (removed before commit): prompt embedded parse ~2.1ms -> cached load_all_prompts ~0.2ms; skill embedded parse ~1.1ms saved/startup; agent embedded parse ~0.2ms saved/startup. Combined ~3.4ms of per-startup CPU removed after the first server in a process.
+- [x] `cargo nextest run --workspace` MCP-server-startup tests pass under load WITHOUT serial guards or the `mcp_server` `slow-timeout` override (both remain absent). Full run: 13772 passed, 1 timed out, 1 skipped. The single timeout is `mcp::file_watcher::tests::test_file_watcher_start_watching_replaces_previous` — a notify/FSEvents test (different root cause, tracked by 01KS3TQQTM404TGYFZ9E39EXP3 / 01KS5B9ZH604586D1AHC3NVPFJ), passes in 7.3s in isolation, unrelated to this cache. No mcp_integration/server-startup timeouts.
+- [x] No behavior change to exposed prompts/skills/agents (precedence preserved).

-**Fix**: parse each embedded builtin library exactly once per process and reuse it cheaply across server instances.
+## Tests
+- [x] Unit tests proving once-per-process parse: `test_embedded_builtins_parsed_once_per_process` in each of skills/agents/prompts resolvers (AtomicUsize counter == 1 after N constructions).
+- [x] Regression test cache does not shadow filesystem: `test_filesystem_prompt_overrides_cached_builtin` (prompts) using `IsolatedTestEnvironment`.
+- [x] Previously-timing-out tests pass fast in isolation: `test_mcp_server_basic_functionality` (13.6s), `test_mcp_server_prompt_loading` (20s), `test_mcp_server_builtin_prompts` (20s), `test_client_list_tools` (7.7s) — all pass.
+- [x] Full-workspace verification: see above. `cargo clippy --workspace`-equivalent on the 3 crates + `cargo fmt` clean (unrelated pre-existing fmt churn in claude-agent reverted to keep diff scoped).

-- Builtin prompts, skills, and agents are compiled-in static data (`include_dir!`/`include_str!`-style embedding) — identical for the life of the process. Memoize the parsed result behind a process-wide `OnceCell`/`once_cell::sync::Lazy` (or `std::sync::OnceLock`), and have each `McpServer` instance clone/share the cached parsed library (e.g. behind an `Arc`) instead of re-resolving.
-- Scope the cache to the **builtin/embedded portion only**. The filesystem layers (user `~/.swissarmyhammer`, local `./.swissarmyhammer`, VFS `load_all`) MUST still be loaded per-instance/per-working-dir, because those vary by HOME and cwd and tests rely on `IsolatedTestEnvironment` swapping HOME. Only the embedded-constant parse is safe to memoize.
-- Verify thread-safety: the cache is read concurrently by many server startups; the memoized value must be immutable (or `Arc`-shared read-only) after first init.
-
-### Files likely involved
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-prompts/src/prompt_resolver.rs` — cache `load_builtin_prompts()` output.
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-skills/src/skill_library.rs` — cache `SkillResolver::resolve_all()` (builtin portion).
-- The agent-library equivalent (find `AgentLibrary::load_defaults` / its resolver).
-- Possibly `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs` if the sharing seam belongs at `McpServer` construction.
+## Verification commands run
+- `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-skills -p swissarmyhammer-agents -p swissarmyhammer-prompts` -> 381 passed.
+- `cargo clippy -p swissarmyhammer-skills -p swissarmyhammer-agents -p swissarmyhammer-prompts --all-targets -- -D warnings` -> clean.
+- `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-cli -E 'test(test_mcp_server_basic_functionality) | test(test_mcp_server_prompt_loading) | test(test_mcp_server_builtin_prompts)'` -> 3 passed.
+- `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-tools -E 'test(test_client_list_tools)'` -> passed.
+- `cargo nextest run --workspace` -> 13772 passed, 1 unrelated file_watcher timeout, 1 skipped.

-### Relationship to the open #test-failure tasks
-These symptom tasks are all the same root cause and should be re-verified (and likely closed) once this lands: `01KS3TQJ6KT96XH5TR5PY84GC8` (mcp_integration trio — already has the `working_dir` isolation fix applied, serial guards + nextest override removed), `01KS3TQCM3GG44GD4419D6CVXB` (done, mcp_integration basic), `01KS57J5F5YJ3QSB34Q139221R` (test_client_list_tools), `01KS57J8PPWDF20CQ9R3ES8XRP` (kanban-app open-board), and possibly `01KS3TQQTM404TGYFZ9E39EXP3` / `01KS5B9ZH604586D1AHC3NVPFJ` (file_watcher / entity watcher — confirm whether they share this cause or are notify-event-driven, which is different).
+## Notes / follow-ups
+- The file_watcher timeout under load is the separate notify-event-driven root cause; re-verify/close 01KS3TQQTM404TGYFZ9E39EXP3 and 01KS5B9ZH604586D1AHC3NVPFJ independently.
+- Symptom tasks now expected fixed: 01KS3TQJ6KT96XH5TR5PY84GC8, 01KS57J5F5YJ3QSB34Q139221R, 01KS57J8PPWDF20CQ9R3ES8XRP — re-verify and close.

-- [ ] The embedded builtin prompt/skill/agent libraries are parsed at most once per process; constructing N `McpServer` instances does not re-parse the embedded data N times (assert via a counter/instrumentation test or a timing test).
-- [ ] Filesystem (user/local/VFS) prompt/skill/agent layers are still loaded per-instance and continue to respect `IsolatedTestEnvironment`'s HOME override — no cross-test bleed of user data.
-- [ ] A single in-process MCP server startup with an isolated `working_dir` measurably drops in CPU/wall-time vs. before (record before/after for one startup).

-- [ ] No behavior change to which prompts/skills/agents a server exposes (builtin + filesystem precedence preserved).
-

-- [ ] Unit test in the cache's home crate proving the embedded library is parsed once: instrument the builtin-parse path with an `AtomicUsize` call counter (test-only) and assert it is 1 after constructing several libraries; OR a `OnceLock`-identity test asserting repeated calls return the same cached `Arc`.
-- [ ] Regression test that an `IsolatedTestEnvironment` with a custom builtin-overriding filesystem prompt still sees its filesystem prompt (cache does not shadow filesystem layers).
-- [ ] Re-run the previously-timing-out tests in isolation to confirm they still pass fast: `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-cli -E 'test(test_mcp_server_basic_functionality) | test(test_mcp_server_prompt_loading) | test(test_mcp_server_builtin_prompts)'` and `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-tools -E 'test(test_client_list_tools)'`.
-- [ ] Full-workspace verification: `cargo nextest run --workspace` completes with zero timeouts in the MCP-server-startup cluster, with the `.config/nextest.toml` `mcp_server` override absent and no `serial_test` guards on those tests. Record honest timings.

+**Fix**: parse each embedded builtin library exactly once per process and reuse it cheaply across server instances.
+
+- Builtin prompts, skills, and agents are compiled-in static data (`include_dir!`/`include_str!`-style embedding) — identical for the life of the process. Memoize the parsed result behind a process-wide `OnceCell`/`once_cell::sync::Lazy` (or `std::sync::OnceLock`), and have each `McpServer` instance clone/share the cached parsed library (e.g. behind an `Arc`) instead of re-resolving.
+- Scope the cache to the **builtin/embedded portion only**. The filesystem layers (user `~/.swissarmyhammer`, local `./.swissarmyhammer`, VFS `load_all`) MUST still be loaded per-instance/per-working-dir, because those vary by HOME and cwd and tests rely on `IsolatedTestEnvironment` swapping HOME. Only the embedded-constant parse is safe to memoize.
+- Verify thread-safety: the cache is read concurrently by many server startups; the memoized value must be immutable (or `Arc`-shared read-only) after first init.

-**Fix (implemented)**: parse each embedded builtin library exactly once per process behind a `once_cell::sync::Lazy`, and have each resolver clone/share the cached parse. Filesystem (user/local/VFS) layers still load per-instance so `IsolatedTestEnvironment` HOME isolation keeps working.
+### Files likely involved
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-prompts/src/prompt_resolver.rs` — cache `load_builtin_prompts()` output.
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-skills/src/skill_library.rs` — cache `SkillResolver::resolve_all()` (builtin portion).
+- The agent-library equivalent (find `AgentLibrary::load_defaults` / its resolver).
+- Possibly `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs` if the sharing seam belongs at `McpServer` construction.

-## Implementation summary
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-skills/src/skill_resolver.rs`: added `static BUILTIN_SKILLS: Lazy<HashMap<String, Skill>>` parsed once via `parse_builtin_skills()`; `load_builtins()` now clones from the cache. Test-only `AtomicUsize` parse counter.
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-agents/src/agent_resolver.rs`: same pattern with `BUILTIN_AGENTS`.
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-prompts/src/prompt_resolver.rs`: added `static BUILTIN_PROMPTS: Lazy<Vec<ResolvedPrompt>>`; extracted `resolve_file_into_prompts()` shared by the cache builder and the filesystem path; `load_all_prompts()` applies cached builtins first (least precedence), then walks only the filesystem VFS and overrides by name. Builtins are no longer added to the per-instance VFS. Added `once_cell` dep.
-- No change to which prompts/skills/agents a server exposes; filesystem-overrides-builtin precedence preserved (verified by new + existing tests).
+### Relationship to the open #test-failure tasks
+These symptom tasks are all the same root cause and should be re-verified (and likely closed) once this lands: `01KS3TQJ6KT96XH5TR5PY84GC8` (mcp_integration trio — already has the `working_dir` isolation fix applied, serial guards + nextest override removed), `01KS3TQCM3GG44GD4419D6CVXB` (done, mcp_integration basic), `01KS57J5F5YJ3QSB34Q139221R` (test_client_list_tools), `01KS57J8PPWDF20CQ9R3ES8XRP` (kanban-app open-board), and possibly `01KS3TQQTM404TGYFZ9E39EXP3` / `01KS5B9ZH604586D1AHC3NVPFJ` (file_watcher / entity watcher — confirm whether they share this cause or are notify-event-driven, which is different).

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [x] Embedded builtin prompt/skill/agent libraries parsed at most once per process (counter tests assert exactly 1 after constructing N resolvers).
-- [x] Filesystem layers still loaded per-instance and respect `IsolatedTestEnvironment` HOME override (new `test_filesystem_prompt_overrides_cached_builtin` proves a user prompt still overrides a cached builtin; existing user/local override tests still pass).
-- [x] Single startup embedded-parse cost measurably drops. Throwaway micro-bench (removed before commit): prompt embedded parse ~2.1ms -> cached load_all_prompts ~0.2ms; skill embedded parse ~1.1ms saved/startup; agent embedded parse ~0.2ms saved/startup. Combined ~3.4ms of per-startup CPU removed after the first server in a process.
-- [x] `cargo nextest run --workspace` MCP-server-startup tests pass under load WITHOUT serial guards or the `mcp_server` `slow-timeout` override (both remain absent). Full run: 13772 passed, 1 timed out, 1 skipped. The single timeout is `mcp::file_watcher::tests::test_file_watcher_start_watching_replaces_previous` — a notify/FSEvents test (different root cause, tracked by 01KS3TQQTM404TGYFZ9E39EXP3 / 01KS5B9ZH604586D1AHC3NVPFJ), passes in 7.3s in isolation, unrelated to this cache. No mcp_integration/server-startup timeouts.
-- [x] No behavior change to exposed prompts/skills/agents (precedence preserved).
+- [ ] The embedded builtin prompt/skill/agent libraries are parsed at most once per process; constructing N `McpServer` instances does not re-parse the embedded data N times (assert via a counter/instrumentation test or a timing test).
+- [ ] Filesystem (user/local/VFS) prompt/skill/agent layers are still loaded per-instance and continue to respect `IsolatedTestEnvironment`'s HOME override — no cross-test bleed of user data.
+- [ ] A single in-process MCP server startup with an isolated `working_dir` measurably drops in CPU/wall-time vs. before (record before/after for one startup).
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run --workspace` completes with the in-process MCP-server-startup tests passing under load **without** per-test `serial_test` guards or the `mcp_server` `slow-timeout` override in `.config/nextest.toml` (i.e. the workarounds stay removed).
+- [ ] No behavior change to which prompts/skills/agents a server exposes (builtin + filesystem precedence preserved).

 ## Tests
-- [x] Unit tests proving once-per-process parse: `test_embedded_builtins_parsed_once_per_process` in each of skills/agents/prompts resolvers (AtomicUsize counter == 1 after N constructions).
-- [x] Regression test cache does not shadow filesystem: `test_filesystem_prompt_overrides_cached_builtin` (prompts) using `IsolatedTestEnvironment`.
-- [x] Previously-timing-out tests pass fast in isolation: `test_mcp_server_basic_functionality` (13.6s), `test_mcp_server_prompt_loading` (20s), `test_mcp_server_builtin_prompts` (20s), `test_client_list_tools` (7.7s) — all pass.
-- [x] Full-workspace verification: see above. `cargo clippy --workspace`-equivalent on the 3 crates + `cargo fmt` clean (unrelated pre-existing fmt churn in claude-agent reverted to keep diff scoped).
+- [ ] Unit test in the cache's home crate proving the embedded library is parsed once: instrument the builtin-parse path with an `AtomicUsize` call counter (test-only) and assert it is 1 after constructing several libraries; OR a `OnceLock`-identity test asserting repeated calls return the same cached `Arc`.
+- [ ] Regression test that an `IsolatedTestEnvironment` with a custom builtin-overriding filesystem prompt still sees its filesystem prompt (cache does not shadow filesystem layers).
+- [ ] Re-run the previously-timing-out tests in isolation to confirm they still pass fast: `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-cli -E 'test(test_mcp_server_basic_functionality) | test(test_mcp_server_prompt_loading) | test(test_mcp_server_builtin_prompts)'` and `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-tools -E 'test(test_client_list_tools)'`.
+- [ ] Full-workspace verification: `cargo nextest run --workspace` completes with zero timeouts in the MCP-server-startup cluster, with the `.config/nextest.toml` `mcp_server` override absent and no `serial_test` guards on those tests. Record honest timings.

-## Verification commands run
-- `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-skills -p swissarmyhammer-agents -p swissarmyhammer-prompts` -> 381 passed.
-- `cargo clippy -p swissarmyhammer-skills -p swissarmyhammer-agents -p swissarmyhammer-prompts --all-targets -- -D warnings` -> clean.
-- `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-cli -E 'test(test_mcp_server_basic_functionality) | test(test_mcp_server_prompt_loading) | test(test_mcp_server_builtin_prompts)'` -> 3 passed.
-- `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-tools -E 'test(test_client_list_tools)'` -> passed.
-- `cargo nextest run --workspace` -> 13772 passed, 1 unrelated file_watcher timeout, 1 skipped.

-## Notes / follow-ups

@@ -42,4 +42,14 @@

 ## Workflow

+- Used `/tdd`: wrote once-per-process parse-count tests + filesystem-override regression test, then introduced the cache, then confirmed symptom tests pass under load with workarounds removed.
+
+## Review Findings (2026-05-21 18:42)
+
+The cache implementation itself is correct, clean, and well-tested — no code-quality blockers. The single finding concerns the causal claim that this cache is what fixes the 300s timeout cluster.
+
+### Warnings
+- [ ] Causal soundness of the timeout fix is UNPROVEN. The implementer's own micro-bench attributes only ~3.4ms of per-startup CPU savings to this cache (prompts ~2.1ms->0.2ms, skills ~1.1ms, agents ~0.2ms), yet the symptom being "fixed" is a 300s nextest hard-kill. ~3.4ms cannot directly explain a 300s->pass transition. A prior full-workspace run that already HAD the working_dir isolation fix but NOT this cache still timed out (basic_functionality 300s, builtin_prompts 300s, prompt_loading 298s), while the single current run with the cache reports the cluster passing (13772 passed). One full-workspace run is insufficient to distinguish "the cache fixed it" from "scheduling/contention variance between runs" — and the acceptance criteria's own intent ("pass under load") implies repeatability. Notably, the linked symptom tasks (01KS3TQQTM404TGYFZ9E39EXP3, 01KS57J5F5YJ3QSB34Q139221R) themselves diagnose these timeouts as OS-level parallel contention (port assignment / HTTP handshake / RMCP client init starving under ~13.7k parallel tests), i.e. NOT CPU-bound builtin parsing — which makes a 3.4ms CPU saving an implausible primary cause. Recommend: (a) run 3+ consecutive `cargo nextest run --workspace` to confirm the cluster reliably passes (matching the multiple-consecutive-runs intent), and (b) if it does NOT reliably pass, investigate the actual dominant per-startup cost — the original investigation likely mis-attributed the magnitude, and the real fix may lie in serializing/limiting concurrency of the in-process-MCP-server tests rather than (or in addition to) this cache. Keep this cache regardless — it is a legitimate, low-risk optimization — but do not close the dependent symptom tasks as "fixed by this cache" until the causal link is demonstrated across multiple runs.
+
+### Nits
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-prompts/src/prompt_resolver.rs:73-80` — `parse_builtin_prompts` swallows builtin parse errors with `tracing::warn!` while the per-instance filesystem path (`load_all_prompts`, line 210) propagates them via `?`. This asymmetry is deliberate (a panic in `Lazy` static init would poison the cache for the whole process) and is documented in the comment, so it is acceptable — but worth a one-line note that builtin parse failures are now strictly non-fatal where, pre-cache, a builtin failure inside the same loop would also have been logged-and-skipped (so behavior is in fact preserved). No action required; flagging only for awareness.
\ No newline at end of file

-## What
+## SUPERSEDED — premise disproven by deeper profiling (2026-05-21)

-**Root cause** (investigated 2026-05-21) of the cluster of `#test-failure` in-process-MCP-server timeouts: every call to `start_mcp_server_with_options` builds a fresh `McpServer` that re-parses the **embedded, identical builtin data** from scratch on every instance (skills/agents/prompts builtin parse). Under full-workspace nextest a tail cluster of concurrent server-startup tests starved CPU and timed out at 300s.
+This task hypothesized that re-parsing embedded builtin prompts/skills/agents per server instance was the cause of the in-process-MCP-server test timeouts. **That is false.** Profiling showed:

-**Fix (implemented)**: parse each embedded builtin library exactly once per process behind a `once_cell::sync::Lazy`, and have each resolver clone/share the cached parse. Filesystem (user/local/VFS) layers still load per-instance so `IsolatedTestEnvironment` HOME isolation keeps working.
+- A full in-process MCP server **builds in ~20ms**; the embedded-builtin parse is ~3.4ms of that. Caching it is noise relative to the timeout.
+- The actual multi-second cost is the **test-side RMCP client HTTP handshake**, dominated by macOS system-proxy resolution on the first request of each fresh `reqwest::Client::default()` (6.4s first request vs 3.2ms with `.no_proxy()`), which serializes through the single-threaded macOS `configd` daemon — explaining the cross-process ~38× degradation.

-## Implementation summary
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-skills/src/skill_resolver.rs`: added `static BUILTIN_SKILLS: Lazy<HashMap<String, Skill>>` parsed once via `parse_builtin_skills()`; `load_builtins()` now clones from the cache. Test-only `AtomicUsize` parse counter.
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-agents/src/agent_resolver.rs`: same pattern with `BUILTIN_AGENTS`.
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-prompts/src/prompt_resolver.rs`: added `static BUILTIN_PROMPTS: Lazy<Vec<ResolvedPrompt>>`; extracted `resolve_file_into_prompts()` shared by the cache builder and the filesystem path; `load_all_prompts()` applies cached builtins first (least precedence), then walks only the filesystem VFS and overrides by name. Builtins are no longer added to the per-instance VFS. Added `once_cell` dep.
-- No change to which prompts/skills/agents a server exposes; filesystem-overrides-builtin precedence preserved (verified by new + existing tests).
+The real fix is tracked in the replacement task. The builtin-library cache implemented for this task is being reverted (333-line prompt_resolver refactor + OnceCell across 3 crates is unjustified complexity for ~3.4ms, and its acceptance criteria — "full-workspace passes without guards" — cannot be met by the cache).

-## Acceptance Criteria
-- [x] Embedded builtin prompt/skill/agent libraries parsed at most once per process (counter tests assert exactly 1 after constructing N resolvers).
-- [x] Filesystem layers still loaded per-instance and respect `IsolatedTestEnvironment` HOME override (new `test_filesystem_prompt_overrides_cached_builtin` proves a user prompt still overrides a cached builtin; existing user/local override tests still pass).
-- [x] Single startup embedded-parse cost measurably drops. Throwaway micro-bench (removed before commit): prompt embedded parse ~2.1ms -> cached load_all_prompts ~0.2ms; skill embedded parse ~1.1ms saved/startup; agent embedded parse ~0.2ms saved/startup. Combined ~3.4ms of per-startup CPU removed after the first server in a process.
-- [x] `cargo nextest run --workspace` MCP-server-startup tests pass under load WITHOUT serial guards or the `mcp_server` `slow-timeout` override (both remain absent). Full run: 13772 passed, 1 timed out, 1 skipped. The single timeout is `mcp::file_watcher::tests::test_file_watcher_start_watching_replaces_previous` — a notify/FSEvents test (different root cause, tracked by 01KS3TQQTM404TGYFZ9E39EXP3 / 01KS5B9ZH604586D1AHC3NVPFJ), passes in 7.3s in isolation, unrelated to this cache. No mcp_integration/server-startup timeouts.
-- [x] No behavior change to exposed prompts/skills/agents (precedence preserved).
+Archiving as superseded. Original description preserved below for history.

-## Tests
-- [x] Unit tests proving once-per-process parse: `test_embedded_builtins_parsed_once_per_process` in each of skills/agents/prompts resolvers (AtomicUsize counter == 1 after N constructions).
-- [x] Regression test cache does not shadow filesystem: `test_filesystem_prompt_overrides_cached_builtin` (prompts) using `IsolatedTestEnvironment`.
-- [x] Previously-timing-out tests pass fast in isolation: `test_mcp_server_basic_functionality` (13.6s), `test_mcp_server_prompt_loading` (20s), `test_mcp_server_builtin_prompts` (20s), `test_client_list_tools` (7.7s) — all pass.
-- [x] Full-workspace verification: see above. `cargo clippy --workspace`-equivalent on the 3 crates + `cargo fmt` clean (unrelated pre-existing fmt churn in claude-agent reverted to keep diff scoped).
+---

-## Verification commands run
-- `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-skills -p swissarmyhammer-agents -p swissarmyhammer-prompts` -> 381 passed.
-- `cargo clippy -p swissarmyhammer-skills -p swissarmyhammer-agents -p swissarmyhammer-prompts --all-targets -- -D warnings` -> clean.

+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-skills/src/skill_resolver.rs`: added `static BUILTIN_SKILLS: Lazy<HashMap<String, Skill>>` parsed once via `parse_builtin_skills()`; `load_builtins()` now clones from the cache. Test-only `AtomicUsize` parse counter.
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-agents/src/agent_resolver.rs`: same pattern with `BUILTIN_AGENTS`.
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-prompts/src/prompt_resolver.rs`: added `static BUILTIN_PROMPTS: Lazy<Vec<ResolvedPrompt>>`; extracted `resolve_file_into_prompts()` shared by the cache builder and the filesystem path; `load_all_prompts()` applies cached builtins first (least precedence), then walks only the filesystem VFS and overrides by name. Builtins are no longer added to the per-instance VFS. Added `once_cell` dep.
+- No change to which prompts/skills/agents a server exposes; filesystem-overrides-builtin precedence preserved (verified by new + existing tests).
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [x] Embedded builtin prompt/skill/agent libraries parsed at most once per process (counter tests assert exactly 1 after constructing N resolvers).
+- [x] Filesystem layers still loaded per-instance and respect `IsolatedTestEnvironment` HOME override (new `test_filesystem_prompt_overrides_cached_builtin` proves a user prompt still overrides a cached builtin; existing user/local override tests still pass).
+- [x] Single startup embedded-parse cost measurably drops. Throwaway micro-bench (removed before commit): prompt embedded parse ~2.1ms -> cached load_all_prompts ~0.2ms; skill embedded parse ~1.1ms saved/startup; agent embedded parse ~0.2ms saved/startup. Combined ~3.4ms of per-startup CPU removed after the first server in a process.
+- [x] `cargo nextest run --workspace` MCP-server-startup tests pass under load WITHOUT serial guards or the `mcp_server` `slow-timeout` override (both remain absent). Full run: 13772 passed, 1 timed out, 1 skipped. The single timeout is `mcp::file_watcher::tests::test_file_watcher_start_watching_replaces_previous` — a notify/FSEvents test (different root cause, tracked by 01KS3TQQTM404TGYFZ9E39EXP3 / 01KS5B9ZH604586D1AHC3NVPFJ), passes in 7.3s in isolation, unrelated to this cache. No mcp_integration/server-startup timeouts.
+- [x] No behavior change to exposed prompts/skills/agents (precedence preserved).
+
+## Tests
+- [x] Unit tests proving once-per-process parse: `test_embedded_builtins_parsed_once_per_process` in each of skills/agents/prompts resolvers (AtomicUsize counter == 1 after N constructions).
+- [x] Regression test cache does not shadow filesystem: `test_filesystem_prompt_overrides_cached_builtin` (prompts) using `IsolatedTestEnvironment`.
+- [x] Previously-timing-out tests pass fast in isolation: `test_mcp_server_basic_functionality` (13.6s), `test_mcp_server_prompt_loading` (20s), `test_mcp_server_builtin_prompts` (20s), `test_client_list_tools` (7.7s) — all pass.
+- [x] Full-workspace verification: see above. `cargo clippy --workspace`-equivalent on the 3 crates + `cargo fmt` clean (unrelated pre-existing fmt churn in claude-agent reverted to keep diff scoped).

-This task hypothesized that re-parsing embedded builtin prompts/skills/agents per server instance was the cause of the in-process-MCP-server test timeouts. **That is false.** Profiling showed:
+## Verification commands run
+- `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-skills -p swissarmyhammer-agents -p swissarmyhammer-prompts` -> 381 passed.
+- `cargo clippy -p swissarmyhammer-skills -p swissarmyhammer-agents -p swissarmyhammer-prompts --all-targets -- -D warnings` -> clean.
+- `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-cli -E 'test(test_mcp_server_basic_functionality) | test(test_mcp_server_prompt_loading) | test(test_mcp_server_builtin_prompts)'` -> 3 passed.
+- `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-tools -E 'test(test_client_list_tools)'` -> passed.
+- `cargo nextest run --workspace` -> 13772 passed, 1 unrelated file_watcher timeout, 1 skipped.

-- A full in-process MCP server **builds in ~20ms**; the embedded-builtin parse is ~3.4ms of that. Caching it is noise relative to the timeout.
-- The actual multi-second cost is the **test-side RMCP client HTTP handshake**, dominated by macOS system-proxy resolution on the first request of each fresh `reqwest::Client::default()` (6.4s first request vs 3.2ms with `.no_proxy()`), which serializes through the single-threaded macOS `configd` daemon — explaining the cross-process ~38× degradation.
+## Notes / follow-ups
+- The file_watcher timeout under load is the separate notify-event-driven root cause; re-verify/close 01KS3TQQTM404TGYFZ9E39EXP3 and 01KS5B9ZH604586D1AHC3NVPFJ independently.
+- Symptom tasks now expected fixed: 01KS3TQJ6KT96XH5TR5PY84GC8, 01KS57J5F5YJ3QSB34Q139221R, 01KS57J8PPWDF20CQ9R3ES8XRP — re-verify and close.

-The real fix is tracked in the replacement task. The builtin-library cache implemented for this task is being reverted (333-line prompt_resolver refactor + OnceCell across 3 crates is unjustified complexity for ~3.4ms, and its acceptance criteria — "full-workspace passes without guards" — cannot be met by the cache).
+## Workflow
+- Used `/tdd`: wrote once-per-process parse-count tests + filesystem-override regression test, then introduced the cache, then confirmed symptom tests pass under load with workarounds removed.

-Archiving as superseded. Original description preserved below for history.
+## Review Findings (2026-05-21 18:42)

----
+The cache implementation itself is correct, clean, and well-tested — no code-quality blockers. The single finding concerns the causal claim that this cache is what fixes the 300s timeout cluster.

-## What
+### Warnings
+- [ ] Causal soundness of the timeout fix is UNPROVEN. The implementer's own micro-bench attributes only ~3.4ms of per-startup CPU savings to this cache (prompts ~2.1ms->0.2ms, skills ~1.1ms, agents ~0.2ms), yet the symptom being "fixed" is a 300s nextest hard-kill. ~3.4ms cannot directly explain a 300s->pass transition. A prior full-workspace run that already HAD the working_dir isolation fix but NOT this cache still timed out (basic_functionality 300s, builtin_prompts 300s, prompt_loading 298s), while the single current run with the cache reports the cluster passing (13772 passed). One full-workspace run is insufficient to distinguish "the cache fixed it" from "scheduling/contention variance between runs" — and the acceptance criteria's own intent ("pass under load") implies repeatability. Notably, the linked symptom tasks (01KS3TQQTM404TGYFZ9E39EXP3, 01KS57J5F5YJ3QSB34Q139221R) themselves diagnose these timeouts as OS-level parallel contention (port assignment / HTTP handshake / RMCP client init starving under ~13.7k parallel tests), i.e. NOT CPU-bound builtin parsing — which makes a 3.4ms CPU saving an implausible primary cause. Recommend: (a) run 3+ consecutive `cargo nextest run --workspace` to confirm the cluster reliably passes (matching the multiple-consecutive-runs intent), and (b) if it does NOT reliably pass, investigate the actual dominant per-startup cost — the original investigation likely mis-attributed the magnitude, and the real fix may lie in serializing/limiting concurrency of the in-process-MCP-server tests rather than (or in addition to) this cache. Keep this cache regardless — it is a legitimate, low-risk optimization — but do not close the dependent symptom tasks as "fixed by this cache" until the causal link is demonstrated across multiple runs.

-(original) Cache embedded builtin libraries once per process to fix slow MCP server startup. See replacement task for the actual root cause and fix.
\ No newline at end of file
+### Nits
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-prompts/src/prompt_resolver.rs:73-80` — `parse_builtin_prompts` swallows builtin parse errors with `tracing::warn!` while the per-instance filesystem path (`load_all_prompts`, line 210) propagates them via `?`. This asymmetry is deliberate (a panic in `Lazy` static init would poison the cache for the whole process) and is documented in the comment, so it is acceptable — but worth a one-line note that builtin parse failures are now strictly non-fatal where, pre-cache, a builtin failure inside the same loop would also have been logged-and-skipped (so behavior is in fact preserved). No action required; flagging only for awareness.
\ No newline at end of file
